diffs.diff:
--- a/model.py
+++ b/model.py
@@ -64,7 +64,7 @@
     def __init__(self, num_classes, low_level_channels=256):

         super(Decoder, self).__init__()

         

-        # Low-level feature processing

+        # Low-level feature processing - ResNet50 layer1 actually outputs 256 channels

         self.low_level_conv = nn.Sequential(

             nn.Conv2d(low_level_channels, 48, kernel_size=1, bias=False),

             nn.BatchNorm2d(48),

@@ -117,6 +117,7 @@
         del self.backbone

         

         # Channel dimensions for ResNet50:

+        # layer0 output: 64 channels

         # layer1 output: 256 channels

         # layer2 output: 512 channels

         # layer3 output: 1024 channels

@@ -133,17 +134,17 @@
         x0 = self.layer0(x)  # [batch, 64, H/2, W/2]

         x = self.maxpool(x0)  # [batch, 64, H/4, W/4]

         x1 = self.layer1(x)  # [batch, 256, H/4, W/4]

-        x2 = self.layer2(x)  # [batch, 512, H/8, W/8]

-        x3 = self.layer3(x)  # [batch, 1024, H/16, W/16]

+        x2 = self.layer2(x1)  # [batch, 512, H/8, W/8]

+        x3 = self.layer3(x2)  # [batch, 1024, H/16, W/16]

         x4 = self.layer4(x3)  # [batch, 2048, H/32, W/32]

         

         # ASPP

-        aspp_features = self.aspp(x4)

+        x = self.aspp(x4)

         

         # Decoder

-        decoded = self.decoder(aspp_features, x1)  # Using x1 as low-level features

+        x = self.decoder(x, x1)  # Using x1 (256 channels) as low-level features

         

         # Upsample to original input size

-        decoded = F.interpolate(decoded, size=x.shape[-2:], mode='bilinear', align_corners=False)

+        x = F.interpolate(x, size=(x0.shape[2]*2, x0.shape[3]*2), mode='bilinear', align_corners=False)

         

-        return decoded
+        return x
